business.headline: Maersk places fresh order for 1,000 India-made shipping containersThe announcement came as Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Sarbananda Sonowal, unveiled the first export-import (EXIM) shipping container manufactured in India for the company at the Maersk-CONCOR Inland Container Depot in UP's Dadri.

E20 even powers F1 cars: Auto experts dismiss concerns, back govt amid backlashIndustry experts defended the rollout of E20 fuel, saying extensive testing found no concerns in compatible vehicles. They said the ethanol push is tied to lower emissions, defined standards and reduced oil dependence.

Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

ITR 2026: Do you need to file two income tax returns? Tax department explainsThe new Income-tax Act has changed the way taxpayers talk about return filing, replacing "Assessment Year" with "Tax Year". But does that mean you'll have to file two ITRs? Here's what the tax department says.

Sensex, Nifty end higher on IT rally; HCLTech surges nearly 6%The BSE Sensex rose 261.79 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 77,763.91, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 95.15 points, or 0.39%, to close at 24,270.85.

IRDAI is finally going after the real reason behind insurance mis-sellingIRDAI is discussing a shift from upfront commissions to staggered payouts with the insurance industry. The proposed changes target mis-selling incentives and could tighten disclosure across distributors.

New EPFO Scheme: Will your employer contribute only Rs 1,800 to PF?The new EPFO Scheme, 2026 has raised an important question among salaried employees: will employers now limit their PF contribution to just Rs 1,800 a month? Here's what the new rules actually say.

Over Rs 9,300 crore lies unclaimed in 31 lakh EPF accounts, enough to build 3 IITsAn RTI response revealed that Rs 9,330 crore remains unclaimed in 30.91 lakh inoperative EPF accounts across India. The disclosure comes as the new EPF Scheme, 2026 takes effect, while details on Aadhaar-linked and high-value accounts remain undisclosed.

Planning to buy a plot in Noida? Read this Noida Authority warning firstThe Noida Authority has warned people against buying or selling land under specified khasra numbers in three villages. It said the parcels are acquired or notified land where illegal construction could bring financial and legal trouble.

Missed your EMI by a day? Here's how it can affect your credit scoreDid you miss your EMI by a day and instantly worry about your CIBIL score? Before you panic, here's what actually happens behind the scenes.

AI mega deal: Why HCLTech shares jump nearly 6% today?At around 11 am, HCLTech was trading nearly 6% higher. The rally also helped lift the broader Nifty IT index for the second straight session, even as AI-heavy markets in South Korea and Taiwan witnessed a sharp selloff.

Air India cuts fuel surcharge on North America, Europe flights as oil easesAir India has reduced temporary fuel surcharges on select long-haul routes from July 1. The move follows easing oil prices after the West Asia conflict had pushed up operating costs.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HCLTech up 4%The BSE Sensex rose 507 points to 78,009.37, while the NSE Nifty50 gained nearly 170 points to trade at 24,346.25 in early trading.

Is the EPFO portal working today? Here's what PF subscribers should knowEPFO has said member and employer services are expected to resume from July 3 after another maintenance extension. The prolonged upgrade has delayed PF claims, passbook access and compliance tasks for nearly a week.

Zero tax in Form 16? You may still have to file an ITR. Here's whyEven if your employer has deducted no income tax because you qualify for the rebate under Section 87A, you may still be legally required to file an ITR in several situations.

EPF Scheme 2026: What employees and employers need to knowWith around eight crore active EPFO subscribers, the government hopes the updated scheme will make provident fund management simpler, more efficient and better suited to a digital-first workplace.

Will petrol, diesel prices be cut? Hardeep Puri says here's when it's possiblePuri explained that although international crude oil prices have fallen in recent weeks, oil companies are still refining crude that was purchased when prices were much higher during the West Asia conflict.

Centre eyes LIC, PSU stake sales to cushion strain from oil crisis: ReportThe Centre is speeding up stake sales in LIC, Hindustan Zinc and other state-run firms. The move is aimed at creating fiscal room as oil price volatility pressures government finances.

Sensex jumps 579 points as IT stocks shine; Nifty closes above 24,175The BSE Sensex climbed 579.48 points, or 0.75%, to close at 77,502.12, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 169.85 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 24,175.70.

AI rally cracks: Why Asian markets are crashing while Indian IT stocks rallyAsian chip stocks slumped after reports stoked fears of excess AI computing capacity. Indian IT shares, more tied to software spending, rose as investors weighed a different AI demand story.

entertainment.headline: Watch: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt exchange wedding vows; dance their heart out A video from Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt's wedding shows them formally accepting each other as husband and wife before family and friends. The clip also captures the newlyweds dancing together after completing their marriage registration in Mumbai.

GDN trailer: R Madhavan brings legendary inventor GD Naidu's story to life The makers of GDN have released the official trailer of the R Madhavan-starrer, offering a glimpse into the inspiring life and struggles of legendary Indian inventor G.D. Naidu. The biographical period drama is directed by Krishnakumar Ramakumar.

Lock Upp 2: Shilpa Shinde joins Farah Khan's show as first wildcard contestant Shilpa Shinde has entered Lock Upp Season 2 as its first wildcard contestant. Her entry comes shortly after she made headlines over her admission regarding the 2017 sexual harassment complaint against producer Sanjay Kohli.

Aamir greets media in 1st public appearance after wedding, wife Gauri Spratt skips Aamir Khan greeted the media after marrying Gauri Spratt at his home. The appearance followed the release of the first official wedding photograph from their private ceremony.

Alliance: Niti Taylor reveals ex-boyfriend burnt her with cigarette, slapped her On Alliance, Niti Taylor said an ex-boyfriend burnt her with a cigarette and slapped her. Her account drew focus to surviving abuse and the vulnerability she felt when she was younger.

Lock Upp 2: Madhuri Grover faces backlash over 'rich should have more kids' remark On Lock Upp 2, Madhuri Jain Grover spoke about wanting a third child and made a remark linking childbirth to wealth and poverty. The comment drew sharp criticism online, overshadowing the episode's eviction and Kangana Ranaut's guest appearance.

First photo: Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt sign marriage papers surrounded by family Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt married at their Bandra home on Sunday morning. The first image from the ceremony shows the couple surrounded by family as they signed the register.

Big B's Jalsa hit by Mumbai rains, flooding outside bungalow goes viral Amitabh Bachchan's iconic bungalow, Jalsa, was surrounded by floodwater as heavy monsoon rains lashed Mumbai, with videos from outside the residence going viral online. It remains unclear whether the actor's customary Sunday darshan will be affected by the waterlogging.

Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are now married LIVE UPDATES Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt got married in a private ceremony at his Bandra home. Their wedding followed a 25-year association and a relationship that began two years ago.

Samay Raina books entire theatre for Alia-Sharvari's Alpha, invites 250 fans Samay Raina has booked an entire Mumbai theatre for an Alpha screening after Alia Bhatt and Sharvari appeared on India's Got Latent. The gesture comes as the film draws mixed reviews, early box office chatter and support from Karan Johar.

Is Naseeruddin Shah right about success of hypermasculine films? Box office verdict A viral clip has revived Naseeruddin Shah's critique of hyper-masculine Indian cinema. Box office figures from 2024 to mid-2026 show alpha heroes still dominate, though softer films have also found audiences.

Alpha box office Day 2: Alia Bhatt's film inches closer to Rs 40 crore worldwide Alia Bhatt's Alpha has inched closer to the Rs 40-crore mark worldwide after registering a modest jump in collections on its first Saturday. The YRF Spy Universe film has now grossed Rs 37.90 crore globally in two days.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan: How delay before release has added to the hype Vijay's Jana Nayagan has kept returning to the spotlight over the months despite getting stuck with censor woes. The delay has only added an element of intrigue.

Pandavani legend Teejan Bai dies at 70, PM Modi pays tribute Teejan Bai, Chhattisgarh's foremost Pandavani singer, died in Raipur after a prolonged illness. Prime Minister Narendra Modi condoled her death on X.

Kyunki Saas... cast reunites after 26 years; Smriti Irani joins celebration. Pics The original cast of Kyunki Saas Bhi Kabhi Bahu Thi, including Smriti Irani, reunited to celebrate the show's 26th anniversary. Hosted by Hiten Tejwani and Gauri Pradhan, the nostalgic get-together delighted fans as the revival series nears its finale.

Akansha Chamola blasts Shreya Kalra after she reveals her bisexuality on Lock Upp Akanksha Chamola confronted Shreya Kalra on Lock Upp after learning her secret had been shared with Sufi Motiwala. The fallout cost her a lifeline and sparked strong support from viewers online.

Lock Upp 2: Sufi Motiwala opens up about the emotional weight of coming out Speaking on Lock Upp Season 2, influencer Sufi Motiwala laid bare the emotional reality of coming out in India. The 21-year-old also reflected on parental expectations, the fear of rejection and why acceptance often takes time.

Salman Khan roots for Sohail as he storms into Alliance as wild card contestant Actor Salman Khan cheered for brother Sohail Khan as he entered Prime Video's Alliance as a wild card contestant. On the show, Sohail opened up about showing his real self and stepping out of his comfort zone.

Actor Sumona Chakravarti reveals endometriosis surgery, shares moving note Actor Sumona Chakravarti revealed that she underwent surgery after her endometriosis progressed despite years of managing the condition. In a heartfelt Instagram post, she also opened up about recovery, online trolling and finding a new purpose on social media.

general news.headline: Married 72 days ago, woman falls to death in Delhi; family alleges murderAkriti Sutar, a 28-year-old newlywed, died after an alleged fall from a Lodhi Colony building on Saturday evening. Her family has accused her husband and in-laws of murder as police begin inquest proceedings.

Never submit to tyranny: Amid war, Karbala message resonates at Khamenei funeralAt Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral in Tehran, mourners linked his death to Imam Hussain's legacy of martyrdom and resistance. Their chants, red flags and invocations of Karbala cast the moment as a living expression of Shia defiance against tyranny.

Mumbai rains claim another life, 63-year-old man killed after tree falls on shopAccording to BMC officials, the incident took place in Mumbai's Kurla West, where Yunus Kundawala sustained critical injuries after being struck by a falling tree.

Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are now married  LIVE UPDATESAamir Khan and Gauri Spratt got married in a private ceremony at his Bandra home. Their wedding followed a 25-year association and a relationship that began two years ago.

The Swades moment: Quiet Ghar Wapsi of India's best facultyFor decades, India's brightest minds left home to shape the world's universities, laboratories and boardrooms. Today, many are returning, not out of nostalgia, but because India's biggest ideas can now be built at home. The country's quiet academic ghar wapsi may well be its most consequential Swadeshi movement yet.

Khamenei's 3 sons pray beside his coffin, his heir Mojtaba remains out of sightThree of Ali Khamenei's sons prayed beside his coffin and four relatives' coffins in Tehran, while successor Mojtaba remained absent. His continued absence, after reported injuries in the February 28 strike, became a focus for mourners as Iran pressed ahead with a week of funeral processions.

Sanju Samson dropped, are you kidding me? Manjrekar stirs the pot after T20I snubSanjay Manjrekar questioned India's call to drop Sanju Samson for the second T20I against England despite handing Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a record debut. The decision and India's defeat sharpened scrutiny on team balance, considering the abundance of left-handers in the batting unit.

No pills, no peace? England deny Viagra claims as noisy Mexican fans ruin sleepFIFA World Cup Round of 16: Thomas Tuchel denied reports that England used Viagra before facing Mexico at the Azteca. The odd exchange came as noisy Mexican fans outside the team hotel added to England's challenge of playing at a higher altitude.

Collapsed lung threatened his JEE dream, but mother's notes helped him reach IIT DelhiWhen Gunjan Kumar was bedridden with pneumothorax before JEE Advanced, his mother attended online classes and made notes for him. Her quiet intervention, alongside his resolve and teachers' guidance, helped the Kota student overcome illness and severe vision loss to move towards IIT Delhi.

Neither civilization, nor honour: Iran hits back after Trump's 'one-shot' threatAli Khamenei's body is currently lying in state in Tehran, where mourners and foreign dignitaries are paying their last respects. The former Supreme Leader, who was killed in a US-Israeli airstrike on February 28, will be buried in Mashhad on July 9 after a week-long funeral procession through Tehran, Qom, Najaf and Karbala.

Government fact-checks reports of Bhutan rejecting India's E20 petrol offerE20 petrol row: The clarification comes days after a report in The Bhutanese claimed that the Bhutan government had asked India to continue supplying conventional petrol for as long as it remained available in the Indian market.

Delhi, Mumbai weather disrupt flights; 15 aircraft diverted, advisory issuedRain and gusty winds disrupted flight operations in Delhi on Saturday evening, forcing 15 flights to divert to nearby airports. Airlines have also issued travel advisories for passengers flying to and from Delhi and Mumbai.

The seven deadly sins didn't disappear. They got corporateThe seven deadly sins may be centuries old, but their modern avatars thrive in today's workplace. From hustle culture and LinkedIn envy to productivity addiction and quiet disengagement, here's how timeless human flaws have evolved to shape corporate life and redefine professional success.

How the mighty have fallen: What went wrong for Germany and ItalyOnce football's gold standard, Germany and Italy are no longer setting the benchmark. Germany lost its edge. Italy lost its way. Both have watched the rest of the football world overtake them. The climb back to the top will demand far more than another man in the dugout.

How Narasimha Rao reset India-US ties in the post-Cold War eraThe year 1991 transformed India in more ways than one. As the country battled an unprecedented economic crisis and adapted to the end of the Cold War, New Delhi simultaneously rewrote its economic policies and foreign policy priorities. The decade that followed witnessed a dramatic shift in India–US relations.

Govt orders Meta to remove child abuse ads on Instagram, seeks reply in 7 daysIssuing a "stern" notice to Meta over paid Instagram ads allegedly promoting child sexual abuse content, the government ordered their removal and sought a detailed explanation from the tech giant within seven days, per ANI sources.

Aamir greets media in 1st public appearance after wedding, wife Gauri Spratt skipsAamir Khan greeted the media after marrying Gauri Spratt at his home. The appearance followed the release of the first official wedding photograph from their private ceremony.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Over 500 applications, no job: Engineering grad turns Rapido rider to make ends meetA first-division Computer Science graduate's story has gone viral after he revealed he became a Rapido rider despite submitting more than 500 job applications. The story has sparked fresh discussion about India's slowing hiring market and the challenges facing new graduates.

Suave in Delhi, steadfast in Chennai: Vijay's quiet federal resetCM Vijay's first two months in office suggest that Tamil Nadu's politics might be entering a new federal phase. While the state's positions on issues such as NEET, language and fiscal devolution remain unchanged, its engagement with the Prime Minister Narendra Modi-led Central government has become markedly more pragmatic.

South Korean First Lady asks price at roadside stall. President's move goes viralSouth Korean president Lee Jae-myung and First Lady Kim Hye-kyung drew attention during a surprise visit to Suwon's Paldalmun Market.

53% of petrol vehicle owners call E20 rollout ineffective or disastrous, claims surveyLocalCircles survey finds 66% of pre-2023 petrol vehicle owners report over 10% drop in mileage, while nearly one-third want the option to buy E0 or E10 petrol even at a higher price.

2 terrorists cornered in orchard in J&K's Shopian, Army plugs escape routesAccording to sources, the gunfight broke out around 7:45 pm in Chanpora village after security forces launched a coordinated anti-terror operation in the area. The Army, police and CRPF tightened the cordon and intensified searches to prevent the terrorists from escaping.

Man finds rain-soaked diary on roadside, every page tells a heartwarming storyA man found a rain-soaked diary found on a roadside and appealed for help tracing its owner. The handwritten keepsake stirred viewers, who hoped the damaged memories could still be recovered.

sports.headline: Not Cape Verde hero, Real Madrid sign Dutch star in right-back position Real Madrid have completed the signing of Netherlands international Denzel Dumfries from Inter Milan, ending speculation over their right-back search. This brings a momentary end to Sidny Lopez Cabral dream of playing under Jose Mourinho for the second time in his career.

No pills, no peace? England deny Viagra claims as noisy Mexican fans ruin sleep FIFA World Cup Round of 16: Thomas Tuchel denied reports that England used Viagra before facing Mexico at the Azteca. The odd exchange came as noisy Mexican fans outside the team hotel added to England's challenge of playing at a higher altitude.

Gurnoor impresses with 10-wicket haul, Sudharsan with twin hundreds vs SL A Fast bowler Gurnoor Brar ripped through Sri Lanka A in the second and final unofficial Test of the tour. India No.3, Sai Sudharsan, playing as an opener in the series, scored twin centuries as well.

Sanju Samson dropped, are you kidding me? Manjrekar stirs the pot after T20I snub Sanjay Manjrekar questioned India's call to drop Sanju Samson for the second T20I against England despite handing Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a record debut. The decision and India's defeat sharpened scrutiny on team balance, considering the abundance of left-handers in the batting unit.

FIFA World Cup: Brazil, England sweat over injuries ahead of Round of 16 games Brazil and England are sweating over key injury concerns ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clashes. Both Carlo Ancelotti and Thomas Tuchel are readying for tough challenges.

Ghost banished: Grigor Dimitrov rewrites Wimbledon script to beat Berrettini Grigor Dimitrov beat Matteo Berrettini in five sets to reach the Wimbledon fourth round. The win completed his comeback from last year's tearful injury exit and revived his hopes of reaching a Slam quarterfinal for the first time in two years.

Brazil play Norway in Round of 16. Will strangest World Cup curse strike again? Brazil are playing Norway in their Round of 16 clash in the FIFA World Cup. The 5-time champions will face a steep test against Erling Haaland and Martin Odegaard's side.

Shan Masood sacked, Babar Azam back: Pakistan's cricket musical chair on again Babar Azam returns as Pakistan's Test captain for the upcoming tours of the West Indies and England, despite a prolonged lean patch. He replaces the sacked Shan Masood, who paid the price for a dismal run of 12 defeats in 16 Tests.

India's mini-slump in T20Is: What's gone wrong with Shreyas Iyer's team? India slumped to their third loss in four matches under the captaincy of Shreyas Iyer. After their defeat in Manchester, Ishan Kishan said that it was important for the team to band together right now.

Like McEnroe: Kapil Dev's candid take on Virat Kohli's aggression, Test retirement Kapil Dev reveals his disappointment over Virat Kohli's Test retirement, suggesting it was fuelled by anger. The 1983 World Cup-winning captain also compared the star batsman's fiery on-field aggression to tennis icon John McEnroe's legendary combative genius.

How the mighty have fallen: What went wrong for Germany and Italy Once football's gold standard, Germany and Italy are no longer setting the benchmark. Germany lost its edge. Italy lost its way. Both have watched the rest of the football world overtake them. The climb back to the top will demand far more than another man in the dugout.

England Women are flying, but can they break the Australia hoodoo in final?Women's T20 World Cup, AUSW vs ENGW: Nat Sciver-Brunt's England and Sophie Molineux's Australia will lock horns in the blockbuster final on Sunday, July 5, at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground.

Mbappe refuses to shake hands, agitated Gill throws ball at France strikerFrance reached the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals after Kylian Mbappe's penalty sealed a 1-0 win over Paraguay, but the match ended with a bizarre confrontation as goalkeeper Orlando Gill threw the ball at the French captain.

Gambhir consoles Samson in long chat after dropping star from T20I teamIND vs ENG: India head coach Gautam Gambhir was seen having a long chat with star opener Sanju Samson during India's 2nd T20I against England. The team management decided to rest Samson after three straight failures and give his spot to the prodigiously talented Vaibhav Sooryavanshi.

Kylian Mbappe: If we have to get our hands dirty, we'll do itFIFA World Cup 2026: France's Kylian Mbappe said that his side are willing to fight dirty if it means victory following their scrappy win against Paraguay in the Round of 16.

Mbappe settles battle of wills as France edge resilient Paraguay into quartersFIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe's second-half penalty finally broke Paraguay's stubborn resistance as France secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory in sweltering conditions to book a World Cup quarter-final against Morocco after an attritional last-16 contest.

PAR 0-1 FRA Highlights: Mbappe's spot kick seals win for France in PhiladelphiaParaguay vs France Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (PAR 0-1 FRA) Mbappe scores from the spot to give France the lead in Philadelphia in a game which had remained deadlocked until the foul on Doue in the box.

I'd rather be us than them: Jesse Marsch defiant despite Canada's World Cup exitFIFA World Cup 2026: Canada's FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign ended with a 3-0 Round-of-16 defeat to Morocco, though Jesse Marsch praised his side's attacking intent despite the result. Morocco advanced to the quarter-finals after a strong second-half display that punished Canada's missed chances.

Morocco shakes off first-half slumber, ends Canada's dream run to reach quartersCanada vs Morocco, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: Azzedine Ounahi struck twice before Soufiane Rahimi added a late third as Morocco shook off a sluggish first half to beat Canada 3-0, ending the co-hosts' historic World Cup run and booking a second straight quarter-final berth.

Shreyas Iyer backs Ravi Bishnoi after costly meltdown in Manchester T20IENG vs IND, 2nd T20I: Ravi Bishnoi had an off day after he leaked 29 runs in the 17th over India lost to England by four wickets on Sunday in Manchester to go 0-1 down in the series.

Iga Swiatek on shock exit after losing to Alexandra Eala: S**t happensDefending champion Iga Swiatek's Wimbledon title defence ended in stunning fashion after a straight-sets defeat to Alexandra Eala, with the Pole admitting that she struggled in the big moments.

Jacob Bethell spoils Sooryavanshi debut party, hands T20 champions third loss-in-a-rowIND vs ENG: Jacob Bethell's unbeaten 76 took England past India's 190 in Manchester after Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's record debut. The chase exposed India's middle-overs slowdown and gave England a 1-0 lead in the five-match series.

CAN 0-3 MAR Highlights: Morocco sink co-hosts Canada to advance to quartersCanada (CAN) vs Morocco (MAR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: Despite Canada being the better side in Houston in the first-half, it's Morocco, who took the lead in Houston through Ounahi. The Moroccan's came out all guns blazing in the second half and late on broke away to triple their lead and put the match to bed.

Watch: Alexandra Eala cries tears of joy after biggest career win over Iga SwiatekAlexandra Eala broke down in tears after upsetting defending champion Iga Swiatek in the third round of Wimbledon to become the first Filipino to reach the fourth round of a Grand Slam.

Heartbroken Serena Williams opts out of Wimbledon doubles: Did everything I couldSerena Williams has withdrawn from Wimbledon 2026 women's doubles, ending her planned reunion with Venus Williams. The knee injury has cut short a closely watched comeback, though Serena suggested it may not be her last appearance.

Iga Swiatek suffers shock Wimbledon exit as Alexandra Eala continues dream runDefending champion Iga Swiatek crashed out of Wimbledon 2026 after World No.32 Alexandra Eala stunned her 7-6(9), 6-2 in a third-round upset on Centre Court.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi promises, but falls cheaply in landmark India debutENG vs IND, 2nd T20I: 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scored 14 runs off nine balls with two sixes before off-spinner Will Jacks dismissed him at the Old Trafford in Manchester.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi becomes India's youngest debutant at 15, beats SachinAt just 15 years, 3 months and 7 days, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player to make an international debut for India. Vaibhav received his maiden cap in the 2nd T20I against England.

Day 6 Highlights: Zverev advances, Anisimova, Swiatek, Rybakina crash outWimbledon Day 6 Highlights: Iga Swiatek has crashed out after losing to Alexandra Eala on Centre on Saturday. Elena Ryabakina has been knocked out after losing to Elise Mertens. Alexander Zverev advanced to the Round of 16, but Amanda Anisimova crashed out.

2nd T20I Highlights: England beat India by 4 wickets after Jacob Bethell 76India (IND) vs England (ENG) 2nd T20I Highlights in Manchester: Jacob Bethell scored an unbeaten 76 as England chased down 191 with six balls to spare at the Old Trafford in Manchester.

Grand Chess Tour: Praggnanandhaa storms into joint lead after in ZagrebR Praggnanandhaa won all three of his games on the final day of the rapid section to catch France's Alireza Firouzja at the top of the standings in the Croatia leg of the Grand Chess Tour. World champion D Gukesh slipped to fifth after drawing all three games.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
